content: 5 PILLARS STOCK SELECTION: 1) Pre-market gappers (>4% gap up). 2) Catalyst (news, earnings, FDA). 3) Float (<100M shares, ideally <20M). 4) Volume (>500K pre-market, >1M by open). 5) Price ($2-$20 range). ALL 5 required. Missing one reduces probability significantly. Gap + catalyst + low float = highest probability. Low float + high volume = explosive moves because limited supply meets high demand.

PULLBACK ENTRY: After initial morning spike (first 5-15 min), wait for pullback to VWAP or 9 EMA. Enter when price bounces off VWAP with increasing volume. Stop below VWAP or recent swing low. Target previous high or breakout level. DON'T CHASE THE SPIKE. Only trade FIRST pullback — second/third have lower probability. First pullback is where institutions reload.

METRICS-DRIVEN IMPROVEMENT: Track EVERY trade. Review daily: win rate, average win/loss, profit factor. Goal: 60-70% win rate with 1:1.5 R:R. Max daily loss: $200 or 2%. Max 3 trades/day when learning. Identify patterns: what time most profitable? Which setup highest win rate? Which to avoid?

LEVEL 2 DATA: Shows bid/ask depth. Large bid orders = support. Large ask orders = resistance. ORDER ABSORPTION = large orders being filled without price moving = someone absorbing all selling (bullish) or buying (bearish). When you see a large bid being absorbed by sellers but it doesn't move = buyer is absorbing all selling pressure = very bullish. Use Level 2 to time entries precisely.

VWAP: Volume Weighted Average Price. Institutional benchmark. Price above VWAP = bullish bias. Below = bearish. VWAP acts as dynamic S/R. In strong trend, price bounces off VWAP repeatedly. First touch after a move = highest probability entry. Second/third touches = diminishing probability. Combine with Level 2 for precision. VWAP bands (1-2 standard deviations) act as extended S/R.

SCALING PLAN: Start 100 shares. After 2 consecutive profitable weeks → 200. After another 2 → 400. Continue doubling. Losing week → back to previous size. NEVER increase after a loss. Psychological benefit: you KNOW you can make money at this size because you've done it. Going 100→200 is easier with 2 weeks of proof.

GAP FILL PROBABILITY: Not all gaps fill. Statistically: small caps with high volume and catalyst gaps are LESS likely to fill (trend day). Large caps with low volume gaps are MORE likely to fill (fade the gap). Morning gap + first candle continuation = trend day (don't fade). Morning gap + immediate reversal in first 5 min = gap fill likely (fade it). Always check if the gap is WITH or AGAINST the higher timeframe trend.

SHORT SELLING RULES: Only short stocks that are up on the day (gapping up then failing). Wait for confirmed lower high on 5m. Enter on break of support with volume. Stop above the lower high. Target VWAP or previous support. Don't short into strength — wait for exhaustion. Small caps can squeeze violently — always use a stop. Short selling has unlimited risk — size accordingly.

MORNING MOMENTUM vs AFTERNOON DRIFT: First 2 hours of session = highest volume, best setups, most momentum. After 11 AM EST = volume drops, setups become lower quality. Afternoon (1-4 PM) = drift mode, low conviction moves. Best rule: make your money in the morning, protect it in the afternoon. If you hit your daily target by 11 AM, STOP. Don't give it back in the afternoon chop.

PRE-MARKET ROUTINE: 1) Scan for gappers >4%. 2) Check catalyst (news, earnings). 3) Verify float and volume. 4) Mark key levels (pre-market high/low, previous day high/low). 5) Set alerts at key levels. 6) Prepare order entries. 7) Review watchlist at market open. The preparation done before market open determines your success. Don't wing it.
